title: Graphic / UX Designer
description: Create visual communications and digital experiences — logos, brand identities, marketing materials, websites, and mobile app interfaces. UX designers focus on user research and product usability.

employability.demand: High — digital economy drives constant demand for design talent.
employability.outlook: Growing; UX and product design especially sought in tech and banking.
pathways: BA/BTech Graphic Design / Visual Communication (3–4 years)
UX bootcamp + portfolio
Agencies, tech companies, in-house brand teams, freelance

title: Journalist / Media Practitioner
description: Research and communicate news, features, and investigations across print, broadcast, online, and social media platforms. Roles span reporter, editor, content strategist, and communications officer.

employability.demand: Moderate — traditional media contracting; digital content and communications growing.
employability.outlook: Restructuring; data journalism, podcasting, and corporate communications expanding.
pathways: BA Journalism / Media Studies (3–4 years)
Cadetships at media houses
SABC, newspapers, online media, PR firms, corporate comms

title: Filmmaker / Animator
description: Create motion-picture content — feature films, documentaries, advertising, and digital animation. Work across production companies, advertising agencies, and as independent creatives.

employability.demand: Moderate — SA film industry is small but growing; animation sector expanding.
employability.outlook: Growing; streaming platforms increasing local content commissioning.
pathways: BA Film / Animation / Media Production (3–4 years)
Production companies, advertising agencies, post-production studios, freelance

title: Fine / Visual Artist
description: Create original works of art — painting, sculpture, printmaking, photography, or mixed media — for exhibition, galleries, commissions, and public art. Often combined with teaching or freelance work.

employability.demand: Moderate — gallery and commission markets; many artists supplement with teaching.
employability.outlook: Stable; digital platforms expanding art sales and audience reach.
pathways: BA Fine Art / BFA (4 years)
Galleries, art schools, community arts projects, commissions, freelance

title: Musician / Sound Engineer
description: Create, perform, or produce music — from live performance to studio recording, composition, and audio post-production. Sound engineers work in recording studios, live venues, and broadcasting.

employability.demand: Moderate — competitive market; streaming and live events drive opportunities.
employability.outlook: Stable; SA music industry growing internationally.
pathways: BA/BMus Music (4 years)
Sound engineering diploma
Music labels, recording studios, broadcasting, live events, freelance performance

title: Public Relations / Communications Specialist
description: Manage an organisation's public image — crafting press releases, managing crises, building media relationships, and developing communication strategies across digital and traditional channels.

employability.demand: High — every large organisation needs communications expertise.
employability.outlook: Growing; social media management and digital PR expanding the role.
pathways: BA Communication / Journalism / PR (3–4 years)
PRISA membership
PR agencies, corporate comms, government, NGOs

title: Photographer / Videographer
description: Capture visual stories — news, portraits, events, products, and documentary imagery. Combine creative and technical skills; work freelance or for media organisations and agencies.

employability.demand: Moderate — highly competitive; specialisation (commercial, medical, sport) improves prospects.
employability.outlook: Stable; content economy keeps demand for visual storytellers alive.
pathways: Diploma / BA in Photography / Media Production (3 years)
Portfolio-led freelance
Media houses, advertising agencies, in-house corporate, freelance

title: Game Developer / Interactive Media Designer
description: Design, code, and produce video games and interactive experiences — from concept and narrative to game mechanics, art direction, and software engineering.

employability.demand: Moderate — SA has a small but growing games industry; remote work enables global studios.
employability.outlook: Growing; mobile games and simulation market expanding regionally.
pathways: BSc Computer Science / BA Game Design (3–4 years)
Portfolio + indie projects
Game studios, interactive media companies, remote global studios
